section.name: BreakTest
section.p: BreakTest is a modern, JMeter-compatible performance testing tool. It started as an independent fork of Apache JMeter and continues the familiar JMX workflow with Java 21+, HTTP/2, lower resource usage, better scheduling, visual debugging, and AI-assisted scripting.
section.h2: What can I do with it?
section.p: BreakTest can test static and dynamic resources, web applications, APIs, databases, queues, files, mail systems, native commands, and custom Java components. It can simulate load against systems you are authorized to test and collect results for debugging, analysis, and reporting.

ul.li:
Ability to load and performance test many different applications/server/protocol types:
Web - HTTP, HTTPS (Java, NodeJS, PHP, ASP.NET, …)
SOAP / REST Webservices
FTP
Database via JDBC
LDAP
Message-oriented middleware (MOM) via JMS
Mail - SMTP(S), POP3(S) and IMAP(S)
Native commands or shell scripts
TCP
Java Objects
Full featured Test IDE that allows fast Test Plan recording (from Browsers or native applications), building and debugging.
CLI mode to load test from Java 21 compatible operating systems.
A complete and ready to present dynamic HTML report
Easy correlation through ability to extract data from most popular response formats, HTML, JSON, XML or any textual format
Complete portability and 100% Java purity.
Modern HTTP runtime with Apache HttpClient 5 and HTTP/2 support.
Lower CPU and memory pressure through lazy response handling, lighter cloning, and listener-driven diagnostics.
Unified Thread Group scheduling with closed and open workload models, phases, pacing, and pause/resume.
Parallel Controller and Fork Controller for realistic concurrent branches of work.
AI-assisted scripting through local agents such as Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and MCP tooling.
Visual debugging with richer View Results Tree details and HAR-backed recorded-vs-replayed request/response diffs.
Caching and offline analysis/replaying of test results.
Pluggable Samplers allow unlimited testing capabilities.
Scriptable Samplers (JSR223-compatible languages like Groovy)
Several load statistics may be chosen with pluggable timers.
Data analysis and visualization plugins allow great extensibility as well as personalization.
Functions can be used to provide dynamic input to a test or provide data manipulation.
Easy Continuous Integration through 3rd party Open Source libraries for Maven, Gradle and Jenkins.
Using BreakTest to understand how to use it
Component reference to have detailed information for every Test element
Functions reference to have detailed information and examples for every function
Properties reference for all properties that allow you to customize BreakTest
Building BreakTest and Add-Ons for advanced usage

section.p: BreakTest is not a browser, it works at protocol level. As far as web-services and remote services are concerned, BreakTest looks like a browser (or rather, multiple browsers); however BreakTest does not perform all the actions supported by browsers. In particular, BreakTest does not execute the Javascript found in HTML pages. Nor does it render the HTML pages as a browser does (it's possible to view the response as HTML etc., but the timings are not included in any samples, and only one sample in one thread is ever displayed at a time).

li.a: Read about existing Issues (Bugs or Enhancements) or reporting new ones (please do it !)

section.p: BreakTest is a derivative work of Apache JMeter and keeps attribution in the source tree, NOTICE file, release notes, and documentation. BreakTest is independent from The Apache Software Foundation and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by The Apache Software Foundation.
